Current distributaries    Waal  [Nederrijn](/wiki/Nederrijn "Nederrijn")  [IJssel](/wiki/IJssel "IJssel")  [Lek](/wiki/Lek_(river) "Lek (river)")  [Merwede](/wiki/Merwede "Merwede")  [Boven Merwede](/wiki/Boven_Merwede "Boven Merwede")  [Nieuwe Merwede](/wiki/Nieuwe_Merwede "Nieuwe Merwede")  [Beneden Merwede](/wiki/Beneden_Merwede "Beneden Merwede")  [Oude Maas](/wiki/Oude_Maas "Oude Maas")  [Dordtsche Kil](/wiki/Dordtsche_Kil "Dordtsche Kil")  [Noord](/wiki/Noord_(river) "Noord (river)")  [Nieuwe Maas](/wiki/Nieuwe_Maas "

([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language") name, pronounced [[ʋaːl]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Waal.ogg "File:Nl-Waal.ogg")) is the main [distributary](/wiki/Distributary "Distributary") branch of the river [Rhine](/wiki/Rhine "Rhine") flowing approximately 80 km (50 mi) through the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"). It is the major waterway connecting the port of [Rotterdam](/wiki/Rotterdam "Rotterdam") to [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Before it reaches Rotterdam, it joins with the [Afgedamde Maas](/wiki/Afgedamde_Maas "Afgedamde Maas") near [Woudrichem](/wiki/Woudrichem "Woudrichem") to form the [Boven Merwede](/wiki/Boven_Merwede "Boven Merwede"). Along its length, [Nijmegen](/wiki/Nijmegen "Nijmegen"), [Tiel](/wiki/Tiel "Tiel"), [Zaltbommel](/wiki/Zaltbommel "Zaltbommel") and [Gorinchem](/wiki/Gorinchem "Gorinchem") are towns of importance with direct access to the river.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b8/View_on_the_waal_janvangoyen.jpg/330px-View_on_the_waal_janvangoyen.jpg)](/wiki/File:View_on_the_waal_janvangoyen.jpg)

The Waal near [Nijmegen](/wiki/Nijmegen "Nijmegen"), 1641

The **Waal** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language") name, pronounced [[ʋaːl]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Waal.ogg "File:Nl-Waal.ogg")) is the main [distributary](/wiki/Distributary "Distributary") branch of the river [Rhine](/wiki/Rhine "Rhine") flowing approximately 80 km (50 mi) through the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"). It is the major waterway connecting the port of [Rotterdam](/wiki/Rotterdam "Rotterdam") to [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Before it reaches Rotterdam, it joins with the [Afgedamde Maas](/wiki/Afgedamde_Maas "Afgedamde Maas") near [Woudrichem](/wiki/Woudrichem "Woudrichem") to form the [Boven Merwede](/wiki/Boven_Merwede "Boven Merwede"). Along its length, [Nijmegen](/wiki/Nijmegen "Nijmegen"), [Tiel](/wiki/Tiel "Tiel"), [Zaltbommel](/wiki/Zaltbommel "Zaltbommel") and [Gorinchem](/wiki/Gorinchem "Gorinchem") are towns of importance with direct access to the river.

The river, which is the main channel in the [Rhine–Meuse–Scheldt delta](/wiki/Rhine%E2%80%93Meuse%E2%80%93Scheldt_delta "Rhine–Meuse–Scheldt delta") system, carries 65% of the total flow of the Rhine.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Waal_(river)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The name Waal, in [Roman](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire") times called *Vacalis, Vahalis* or *Valis*, later *Vahal*, is of [Germanic](/wiki/Germanic_languages "Germanic languages") origin and is named after the many [meanders](/wiki/Meander "Meander") in the river ([West Germanic languages](/wiki/West_Germanic_languages "West Germanic languages"): *wôh*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'crooked'). It is, in turn, thought to have inspired early Dutch [settlers](/wiki/Settler "Settler") of the [Hudson Valley](/wiki/Hudson_Valley "Hudson Valley") region in [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") to name the [Wallkill River](/wiki/Wallkill_River "Wallkill River") after it (*Waalkil* "Waal [Creek](/wiki/Stream "Stream")").

The current river shows little signs of these great bends, since it has been the subject of numerous normalisation projects carried out between the 18th and 20th centuries to improve the river as an economically important shipping route. Some of the cut-off bends are still visible near the main river and are sometimes reconnected to it in times of high water levels.

### Bend in South Holland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Waal_(river)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Bend in South Holland")]

In the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages"), the name "Waal" continued after the confluence with the [Meuse](/wiki/Meuse "Meuse"). The delta parts now known as Boven Merwede, [Beneden Merwede](/wiki/Beneden_Merwede "Beneden Merwede") and the upper section of the [Noord](/wiki/Noord_(river) "Noord (river)") were also called Waal. Near [Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht](/wiki/Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht "Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht"), the mainstream continued west until it flowed into [Oude Maas](/wiki/Oude_Maas "Oude Maas") near [Heerjansdam](/wiki/Heerjansdam "Heerjansdam"). This last stretch past Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht, which separated the [river islands](/wiki/Island "Island") of [IJsselmonde](/wiki/IJsselmonde_(island) "IJsselmonde (island)") and [Zwijndrechtse Waard](/wiki/IJsselmonde_(island)#Zwijndrechtse_Waard "IJsselmonde (island)"), is still called Waal,[[1]](#cite_note-1) but is more commonly known as [Waaltje](/wiki/Waaltje "Waaltje") (Dutch for *Little Waal*). It has been dammed off at both ends, making the Zwijndrechtse Waard part of IJsselmonde.

### Rail bridges

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Waal_(river)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Rail bridges")]

Showing nearest station on the left and right bank:

* [Nijmegen railway bridge](/wiki/Nijmegen_railway_bridge "Nijmegen railway bridge"), between [Nijmegen](/wiki/Nijmegen_railway_station "Nijmegen railway station") and  [Lent](/wiki/Nijmegen_Lent_railway_station "Nijmegen Lent railway station").[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Dr. W. Hupkes Bridge](/w/index.php?title=Dr._W._Hupkes_Bridge&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dr. W. Hupkes Bridge (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dr._W._Hupkesbrug "nl:Dr. W. Hupkesbrug")], between [Zaltbommel](/wiki/Zaltbommel_railway_station "Zaltbommel railway station") and [Geldermalsen](/wiki/Geldermalsen_railway_station "Geldermalsen railway station").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Water quality

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Waal_(river)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Water quality")]

The Waal has significant adverse water quality due to discharge of raw [sewage](/wiki/Sewage "Sewage") by [France](/wiki/France "France") and [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). A number of pathogens have been monitored to occur in the river waters from such sewage.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
